insights: Facsimile signatures are the primary rejection driver (2/6 = 33% of rejections); confirmed listings avoid printed/mass-produced signature markers.
Title-card mismatches and wrong parallel/year combinations rejected 2/6 listings; confirmations have matching titles and accurate set years.
Confirmed snipes feature genuine autographs from established sets (Topps Tribute, Authentic Images) with >80% price delta vs. market; rejections mixed novelty/facsimile products.
Emojis and hype language (🔥🚀⭐️) correlate with rejections; confirmed listings use neutral, descriptive titles with card numbers and parallels.
Low-quality/vague titles ('Group of noted Baseball Collecting Cards') indicate wrong_card_num rejections; real snipes specify exact card numbers and years.
2024+ Panini Donruss entries rejected (wrong_set + wrong_card_num); market favors vintage/modern Topps autographs with clear authentication.
Rejection reason_code distribution: facsimile_signature=2, wrong_card_num=2, other=1, wrong_parallel=1; no single dominant error suggests need for multi-layered blocklist.
Confirmed listings leverage underpriced vintage/modern reprints with clear pedigree; rejections attempt to pass facsimiles or misrepresented parallels as real pulls.
